examples.title: Requirements Prioritization using MoSCoW
examples.description: Algorithm to categorize and prioritize requirements based on stakeholder priority levels
examples.code:
// Requirements Prioritization Algorithm
BEGIN
 INPUT: List of Requirements R[], Stakeholder Priorities P[]
 OUTPUT: Categorized Requirements (Must, Should, Could, Won't)

 FUNCTION categorizeRequirements(R, P):
 mustList = []
 shouldList = []
 couldList = []
 wontList = []

 FOR each req IN R:
 priorityScore = calculatePriority(req, P)

 IF priorityScore >= 9 THEN
 mustList.add(req)
 ELSE IF priorityScore >= 7 THEN
 shouldList.add(req)
 ELSE IF priorityScore >= 4 THEN
 couldList.add(req)
 ELSE
 wontList.add(req)

 RETURN {mustList, shouldList, couldList, wontList}

 FUNCTION calculatePriority(req, P):
 // Weighted priority based on stakeholder importance
 score = 0
 FOR each stakeholder IN P:
 IF req.fulfillsNeed(stakeholder) THEN
 score = score + (stakeholder.weight * req.urgency)
 RETURN score / totalWeight
END
examples.explanation: This algorithm categorizes requirements into MoSCoW priorities based on weighted stakeholder needs. Higher weighted stakeholders have more impact on priority scores.

examples.title: Stakeholder Analysis Matrix
examples.description: Algorithm to analyze and classify stakeholders for negotiation planning
examples.code:
// Stakeholder Analysis Algorithm
BEGIN
 INPUT: Stakeholders S[], Project Requirements R[]
 OUTPUT: Engagement Strategy for each stakeholder

 FUNCTION analyzeStakeholders(S, R):
 engagementPlan = {}

 FOR each stakeholder IN S:
 power = stakeholder.evaluatePower() // 1-5 scale
 interest = stakeholder.evaluateInterest() // 1-5 scale

 IF power >= 3 AND interest >= 3 THEN
 strategy = "MANAGE_CLOSELY"
 frequency = "Weekly"
 ELSE IF power >= 3 AND interest < 3 THEN
 strategy = "KEEP_SATISFIED"
 frequency = "Bi-weekly"
 ELSE IF power < 3 AND interest >= 3 THEN
 strategy = "KEEP_INFORMED"
 frequency = "Monthly"
 ELSE
 strategy = "MONITOR"
 frequency = "Quarterly"

 engagementPlan[stakeholder] = {strategy, frequency}

 RETURN engagementPlan

 FUNCTION evaluateBATNA(stakeholder, alternatives[]):
 // Calculate Best Alternative To Negotiated Agreement
 bestValue = 0
 FOR each alt IN alternatives:
 value = stakeholder.evaluateOption(alt)
 IF value > bestValue THEN
 bestValue = value
 RETURN bestValue
END
examples.explanation: This algorithm classifies stakeholders based on power and interest to determine appropriate engagement strategies and calculates BATNA for negotiation preparation.
